tasks.label: Set up project (macOS/Linux)
tasks.command: python3 -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/python -m pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/python -m pip install -r requirements.txt && echo '' && echo '========================================================' && echo '  Setup complete!' && echo '' && echo '  NEXT STEP: tell VS Code to use the new environment.' && echo '    1. Look at the bottom-right corner of VS Code' && echo '    2. Click the Python version shown there' && echo '    3. Pick the one labelled  .venv  (Recommended)' && echo '' && echo '  Then open a NEW terminal — it will show (.venv)' && echo '  in the prompt. You are good to go.' && echo '========================================================'

tasks.label: Set up project (Windows)
tasks.command: py -m venv .venv && .venv\Scripts\python -m pip install --upgrade pip && .venv\Scripts\python -m pip install -r requirements.txt && echo. && echo ======================================================== && echo   Setup complete! && echo. && echo   NEXT STEP: tell VS Code to use the new environment. && echo     1. Look at the bottom-right corner of VS Code && echo     2. Click the Python version shown there && echo     3. Pick the one labelled  .venv  (Recommended) && echo. && echo   Then open a NEW terminal -- it will show (.venv) && echo   in the prompt. You are good to go. && echo ========================================================
